author: codex-agent

patch.value:
Research snapshot (2026-05-17):
- Existing init flow lives in src/cli/commands/init.ts and already owns directory creation, settings writes, TTY-only wizard prompts, --defaults, --author, --with-packages, runtime schema scaffolding, package installation, and next_steps output.
- Command wiring for init is in src/cli/register-setup.ts. Help examples/tips are in src/cli/help-content.ts. SDK/MCP action contracts expose init optional keys from src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts. JSON key-order regression for init is covered in tests/integration/release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts.
- Settings schema/types currently have telemetry prompt state but no project-level agent-guidance state. Add a narrow settings.agent_guidance object for prompt completion, decline, template version, and last checked files. Unknown settings are stripped today, so schema/types/serialization must be updated together.
- AGENTS.md exists in this repo and already contains extensive pm guidance; CLAUDE.md is absent. The feature should work generically for either root AGENTS.md, root CLAUDE.md, both, or neither.
- Existing docs already define the content source: docs/AGENT_GUIDE.md, docs/COMMANDS.md, docs/QUICKSTART.md, and guide topic workflows. The init-injected block should be shorter than this repo AGENTS.md and idempotent, with stable markers and a template version.

Implementation direction:
1. Add an idempotent detector/patcher for AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md that checks for a stable pm guidance marker plus core command tokens such as pm init, pm context, pm search, pm create, pm claim, pm files/docs/test, pm test --run, pm close, pm release, and PM_AUTHOR.
2. Extend pm init with explicit modes, preferably a single value flag such as --agent-guidance <ask|add|skip|status> plus a convenience alias if desired. Default ask mode should prompt only in TTY and only when guidance is missing and the project has not declined.
3. On approval, append or create a clearly marked section without clobbering user content. On decline, write project settings so the user/agent is not asked again. A later pm init --agent-guidance add should override the decline and write the section.
4. Non-interactive init should never block. It should report guidance_missing in next_steps/warnings unless --agent-guidance add/skip/status makes intent explicit.
5. Update help, contracts, config/settings docs, tests, and plugin/guide surfaces so agents can discover the workflow.
Add pm init checks for AGENTS/CLAUDE pm workflow guidance

Teach pm init to detect whether AGENTS.md and/or CLAUDE.md include core pm command/workflow guidance, ask before adding missing guidance, persist declined prompts, and expose an explicit init option to add the guidance later.

pm init detects missing core pm guidance in AGENTS.md and/or CLAUDE.md; TTY init asks before modifying files; approval writes idempotent marked guidance; decline persists project state so future init runs do not ask; explicit pm init option can add guidance later; non-interactive runs do not block; settings/schema/contracts/help/docs/tests cover the behavior.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: ran pm context --limit 10; pm search "AGENTS CLAUDE init workflow help" --limit 10; pm list-open --limit 20; pm list-in-progress --limit 20; inspected pm-8wwl and pm-v7dj. No existing active item covers init-time AGENTS/CLAUDE guidance detection, approval, decline persistence, and later explicit add.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Reuse the existing TTY-only init wizard pattern and project settings persistence instead of creating a second prompt store. Keep context-file edits idempotent with stable markers so repeated init runs are safe.
